description: Temporary-controlled-activation acceptance scenario: the SLA Watchdog workflow (05) is invoked once via its 'Execute Workflow Trigger (Synthetic Test Entry)' while the hmz-send-state sidecar reports one unfinished send aged past the breach threshold. The run is performed in the same temporary-activation window used by run-local-runtime-acceptance.ps1 (Reply Sender / Error Handler / Human Approval) and the workflow is restored to active=false in the finally block regardless of outcome. config.review.google_chat_configured remains false in this profile, so the notification step must fail closed without attempting an external POST.

Immediately re-running the same temporary-activation invocation with the identical unresolved sendKey must be deduplicated by the F. Dedupe Alerts (hmz-send-state) step: the alert is recognised as already raised and is not re-counted as a new alert, proving the watchdog does not produce duplicate notifications across repeated polls within its dedupe window.
